page: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 64 dedicated physical cores based on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, specifically utilizing the Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 34 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory subsystem throughput. The instance achieves top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, memory bandwidth for both cached small block and uncached large block reads and writes, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also demonstrates top-tier results in PassMark memory tests, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU encryption tests falling into the middle 50% average tier.

The hardware profile presents specific tradeoffs, particularly in machine learning workloads. While prompt processing for a medium 7B LLM model registers as strong, text generation for a small 135M model is poor, falling into the bottom 10%. This indicates the instance is less suited for real-time text generation tasks. Given the high multi-core efficiency, balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, and top-tier database and web serving benchmarks, the m9g.16xlarge is suitable for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, Redis caching layers, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 34 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are more moderate. This hardware profile is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis caching, database hosting, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 34 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores running at 2.8 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Moderate single-core performance and poor low-parameter LLM text generation speeds
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, Redis caching, and static web serving
